violations.claim: REACHABLE: trivialCount understates true count of trivial identity proofs
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 137
; REACHABLE: trivialCount understates true count of trivial identity proofs
(declare-const non_vacuous_count Int)
(declare-const truly_trivial_non_vacuous Int)
(declare-const is_trivial_detected Int)
(declare-const trivial_count Int)
; Transition: results contains non_vacuous_count entries (vacuous blocks already dropped)
(assert (>= non_vacuous_count 1))
; Transition: truly_trivial_non_vacuous blocks have the form (assert (= A B)) + (assert (not (= A B)))
(assert (>= truly_trivial_non_vacuous 1))
(assert (<= truly_trivial_non_vacuous non_vacuous_count))
; From existing violation: isTrivialIdentity regex matches only atomic equalities of the form
; (assert (= <atom> <atom>)) — compound expressions like (assert (= (+ a b) c)) are missed
(assert (>= is_trivial_detected 0))
(assert (< is_trivial_detected truly_trivial_non_vacuous))
; Transition: trivial_count = results.filter(r => r.trivial).length = is_trivial_detected
(assert (= trivial_count is_trivial_detected))
; Violation: log "N trivial identity proofs flagged" understates actual trivial count
(assert (< trivial_count truly_trivial_non_vacuous))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: REACHABLE: return value [] is ambiguous between "no SMT2 blocks" and "all blocks vacuous"
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 137
; REACHABLE: return value [] is ambiguous between "no SMT2 blocks" and "all blocks vacuous"
(declare-const scenario_a_results Int)   ; Scenario A: extractSmt2Blocks returned []
(declare-const scenario_b_total Int)     ; Scenario B: N blocks all vacuous
(declare-const scenario_b_vacuous Int)
(declare-const scenario_b_results Int)
; Scenario A transition: response contains no fenced SMT2 blocks → results = []
(assert (= scenario_a_results 0))
; Scenario B transition: response contains N genuine SMT2 blocks, all vacuous
(assert (>= scenario_b_total 1))
(assert (= scenario_b_vacuous scenario_b_total))
; Transition: nonVacuous = [] → results = [] in Scenario B
(assert (= scenario_b_results (- scenario_b_total scenario_b_vacuous)))
; Violation: both scenarios produce identical return values
; caller cannot distinguish "nothing to verify" from "everything was garbage"
(assert (= scenario_a_results scenario_b_results))
(check-sat)
